title: The Relic Battery
intro: A trader leaves a strange thing in pawn: a pre-Burning relic of rune-etched brass, still faintly warm — a battery, the coven decides, built to store a great charge of mana. Getting the charge out without ruining the relic is a matter of the old craft.

advisors.text: It is good work, Archon — old, practical, and full. Coax it right and it pours. Force it and we get a fistful and a cracked shell.

win.text: Your craft is equal to the old maker's. The relic gives up its whole charge in a clean, gorgeous flood, and survives to be charged again.
win.saga: The coven's craft unlocked a pre-Burning mana-battery cleanly; it poured out its whole charge and survived whole.

lose.text: The old bindings are subtler than they looked. The relic cracks as it discharges — a fair draught of mana, and a ruined, worthless shell.
lose.saga: The coven cracked a pre-Burning mana-battery drawing its charge; some mana came, and a priceless relic was ruined.

choices.label: Study it a season before touching it

win.text: You leave the charge alone and study the relic's making instead — a season's careful notes, and a little mana teased safely from its surface.
win.saga: You studied a pre-Burning mana-battery for a season before daring its charge, gaining lore and a little mana.
